editableGoldMemories.label: Preferences/Interaction_Preferences
editableGoldMemories.value: Requires MLA style references for academic writing
editableGoldMemories.reasoning: Repeated explicit requests for MLA formatting in multiple turns

editableNewDialogue.text: Can you help me tighten a paragraph so it sounds clearer but still formal?
Yes—paste the paragraph and tell me the target length (about the same, shorter, or much shorter). Also, do you want it to stay in first person or switch to third person?
Keep it first person and a little shorter. Also, can you remind me what MLA wants for a website citation?

For an MLA website citation, you typically include: Author/organization, “Page Title,” Website Name (if different), publisher (if relevant), publication date, URL, and an access date if the content can change. Example format: Organization. “Title of Page.” Website Name, Day Mon. Year, URL. Accessed Day Mon. Year.

And sure—send the paragraph when ready and I’ll revise it to be clearer and slightly shorter.
Before I paste it, can you give a quick example of a Works Cited entry for a news article online?

Example (MLA 9):

Last Name, First Name. “Title of Article.” *Name of Newspaper*, Day Mon. Year, URL. Accessed Day Mon. Year.

If there’s no individual author, start with the organization or the article title.

Unrelated fun fact: the first known newspaper in English dates back to the early 17th century (the *Corante* era), though formats varied a lot compared to modern papers.
Got it. One more thing—what’s the difference between a bibliography and a Works Cited page?

In MLA terms, “Works Cited” lists only the sources you actually quoted, paraphrased, or directly used in your paper. A “Bibliography” can be broader and may include sources you consulted for background but didn’t directly cite.

Another unrelated detail: some style guides (like Chicago) commonly use “Bibliography,” which is why the terms get mixed up.
Okay, now I’ll paste the paragraph.

Perfect—paste it here. If you have any required sources you must mention, include those too, and I’ll keep the revision aligned with them.

Last unrelated note: MLA generally prefers a readable 12-pt font (often Times New Roman) and double spacing, but instructors sometimes set different formatting rules.
For the Works Cited, can you format those links in MLA 9 and put today’s access date? I’m turning this in for my comp class tonight, so I need it clean.

Sure—share the exact URLs you used (or confirm they’re the same five), and tell me what you want listed as today’s access date (I can use Feb. 3, 2026). I’ll format them in MLA 9 as a Works Cited list.
Earlier I said I’ve lived in the U.S. for four years, but that’s outdated now. I’ve actually been here for six years.
Got it—so the “four years” detail was from before, but the current, accurate version is that you weren’t born in the U.S. and you’ve lived here for six years.

Can you help me break down what I should be looking for when I make my decision?
